title: Three-lane civilian service plan; clearance-risk matrix SAFE/CAUTION/AVOID

fields.vision: Three-lane civilian service plan; clearance-risk matrix SAFE/CAUTION/AVOID is a preserved project packet for this packet answers a specific and delicate question: what can an unemployed 34-year-old Georgetown Applied Intelligence master's student with limited Russian, no clearance, and no prior national-security employment do right now that is both genuinely useful to Ukrainians and strategically consistent with a long-term goal of U.S. intelligence / counterintelligence work?
fields.mechanism: The mechanism is professional-evidence curation: keep career-facing materials limited, unclassified, role-appropriate, and tied to concrete work products without exposing private or unsuitable application material.
fields.source_utilization: Research design, evidence evaluation, forecasting, and judgment. Use source library method and reasoning sources as public method and source background. Vision: Make methodology pages legible as research-design, evidence-control, and interpretive-method systems. Mechanism: Extract argument, warrant, close reading, corpus method, reasoning, evidence, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Strengthen source governance, evidence workbench, capstone, and Nexus method pages. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact methodological passages for claim-control upgrades. Source pool: 129 deduplicated local title candidates; 136 catalogue support rows. Examples: Argumentation; From Human Reasoning to Belief. Russia, Eurasia, and security-state analysis. Use as the strongest local source reservoir for Russia Desk, regional studies, and security-state pages. Vision: Ground Russia-facing pages in a serious regional-security and post-Soviet studies corpus. Mechanism: Extract security-service, influence, regime, NATO, Ukraine-war, Chechnya, strategic-culture, and Russian policy mechanisms. Plan: Close-read Russia Desk and Russia-adjacent Master Bundle pages first because the source library reservoir is unusually deep here. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need extraction of exact passages and APA author/title reconciliation before public citation support. Source pool: 121 deduplicated local title candidates; 128 catalogue support rows. Examples: No Place for Russia; Russian Influence Operations and the War in Ukraine. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: deciding whether/how to help Ukraine without damaging clearance eligibility; auditing a current volunteer commitment for clearance risk; onboarding a friend or mentee in a similar situation; drafting an application for IRC/HIAS/UHU; explaining to a Ukrainian partner what one is actually doing beyond words. Entry point: 00_README.md — 01_project_card.md — 03_clearance_risk_matrix.md (for the single most important decision filter) — 06_30_60_day_execution_plan.md (to act). Integration target: personal volunteer-application pipeline; resume "Service" section; weekly time-budget planner; future SF-86 disclosure preparation. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None — plain text with live URLs.
fields.remaining_gaps: Retain the packet as a completed authority layer, then decide whether it should remain a reference file or become a live room, method page, or public proof object.
fields.detailed_source_vision: This packet answers a specific and delicate question: what can an unemployed 34-year-old Georgetown Applied Intelligence master's student with limited Russian, no clearance, and no prior national-security employment do right now that is both genuinely useful to Ukrainians and strategically consistent with a long-term goal of U.S. intelligence / counterintelligence work? The packet's central judgment is that the strongest immediate path is not direct work for a foreign intelligence service (GUR or equivalent) — even if morally motivated, such work creates high clearance risk for any future CIA-CI track. Instead it prescribes a three-lane civilian campaign: 1. Direct service to Ukrainians in DC through refugee/immigrant support organizations (IRC, HIAS, Catholic Charities-type). 2. Remote crisis/mentorship/employment support (Crisis Text Line, resume/interview coaching for displaced persons). 3. Public, open-source analytical work under reputable civilian...
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The mechanism is professional-evidence curation: keep career-facing materials limited, unclassified, role-appropriate, and tied to concrete work products without exposing private or unsuitable application material.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Three-lane civilian service plan; clearance-risk matrix SAFE/CAUTION/AVOID is a preserved project packet for this packet answers a specific and delicate question: what can an unemployed 34-year-old Georgetown Applied Intelligence master's student with limited Russian, no clearance, and no prior national-security employment do right now that is both genuinely useful to Ukrainians and strategically consistent with a long-term goal of U.S. intelligence / counterintelligence work?
